query: What are the top AI news headlines today? Include sources.
textResponse:
Here are some of the biggest **AI headlines for Thursday, April 9, 2026** that surfaced in the last couple of days:

1. **OpenAI publishes a U.S. child-safety AI policy blueprint** — OpenAI announced a new policy framework focused on strengthening child protection in the age of AI, published April 7. ([openai.com](https://openai.com/index/introducing-child-safety-blueprint/?utm_source=openai))

2. **OpenAI says enterprise AI adoption is accelerating faster than expected** — In a post published April 8, OpenAI described its “next phase of enterprise AI,” arguing the business rollout of AI is speeding up. ([openai.com](https://openai.com/index/next-phase-of-enterprise-ai/?utm_source=openai))

3. **Meta launches “Muse Spark” in a major reboot of its AI efforts** — TechCrunch reports Meta released a new model, Muse Spark, on April 8 as the first model from Meta Superintelligence Labs and part of a broader overhaul of Meta’s AI strategy. ([techcrunch.com](https://techcrunch.com/2026/04/08/meta-debuts-the-muse-spark-model-in-a-ground-up-overhaul-of-its-ai/?utm_source=openai))

4. **Anthropic expands compute capacity with Google and Broadcom** — TechCrunch reports Anthropic signed a new agreement to secure more processing power for Claude amid surging demand. The story was published April 7. ([techcrunch.com](https://techcrunch.com/2026/04/07/anthropic-compute-deal-google-broadcom-tpus/?utm_source=openai))

5. **Chinese startup Z.ai releases open-source GLM-5.1 model** — VentureBeat reports the model is MIT-licensed and positioned for long-running autonomous tasks, with claims of strong SWE-Bench Pro performance. Published April 7. ([venturebeat.com](https://venturebeat.com/technology/ai-joins-the-8-hour-work-day-as-glm-ships-5-1-open-source-llm-beating-opus-4?utm_source=openai))

6. **Microsoft rolls out three in-house AI models** — VentureBeat reports Microsoft launched new transcription, voice, and image models on April 3, signaling a stronger direct push against OpenAI and Google. ([venturebeat.com](https://venturebeat.com/technology/microsoft-launches-3-new-ai-models-in-direct-shot-at-openai-and-google?utm_source=openai))

If you want, I can also turn this into a **more polished “morning briefing”** with:
- a **5-bullet TL;DR**
- **why each headline matters**
- and **links grouped by company**.
